title: Cardiovascular Compromise in the Newborn Infant

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Cardiovascular Compromise?
5 take-home pearls ที่ต้องจำก่อนอ่านบทนี้: (1) Shock ใน neonate diagnose ยาก เพราะ BP ≠ blood flow โดยเฉพาะช่วง transitional period — BP ปกติไม่ได้แปลว่า hemodynamics ดี; (2) Compensated shock: BP ปกติ แต่ organ perfusion ลด → ต้องดู urine output, lactate, NIRS; (3) ≤50% VLBW มี BP ต่ำใน transitional period แต่ไม่ใช่ทุกรายที่ต้องรักษา — ต้องประเมิน context; (4) Treatment-based hierarchy: volume → dopamine → dobutamine → hydrocortisone (vasopressor-resistant); (5) EPIPAGE 2 (2017): รักษา asymptomatic hypotension → ↓IVH, ↑survival without severe morbidity.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย oxygen delivery formula และ determinants ของ cardiac output
แยก compensated vs uncompensated vs irreversible shock
รู้ etiologies: hypovolemia / myocardial dysfunction / abnormal vasoregulation
ใช้ Nuntnarumit nomogram ประเมิน hypotension ตาม GA/postnatal age
รู้ limitations ของ clinical signs (CRT, urine output, lactate)
อธิบาย role ของ SVC flow และ NIRS ใน hemodynamic assessment
รู้ volume resuscitation: isotonic saline 10-20 mL/kg, indication
รู้ dopamine pharmacology (dose-dependent receptors), dobutamine (cardioselective), epinephrine
รู้ hydrocortisone rationale ใน vasopressor-resistant hypotension
รู้ ECMO indication (OI >40, refractory shock)
1. Principles of Oxygen Delivery
1.1 DO2 Formula
DO2 = CO × CaO2; CO = HR × SV; CaO2 = (1.34 × Hb × SaO2) + (0.003 × PaO2)
VO2 = CO × (CaO2 − CvO2) [Fick principle]
Normal O2 extraction ~25%; SaO2 100% → SvO2 75%
Critical DO2 threshold → anaerobic metabolism → lactate ↑
Aerobic: 38 ATP/glucose; Anaerobic: 2 ATP + 2 lactate/glucose
1.2 Cardiac Output Determinants
Preload: end-diastolic volume; Frank-Starling relationship
Afterload: force ventricle must overcome; ↑afterload → ↓CO
Contractility: intrinsic force per unit time; immature neonatal myocardium especially sensitive
Neonatal CO: more heart-rate dependent than contractility (limited SV augmentation)
1.3 Qp:Qs Calculation
Qp:Qs = (SaO2 − SvO2)/(SpvO2 − SpaO2); assumes SpvO2 = 95-100%
HLHS example: SaO2 95%, SvO2 80% → Qp:Qs = 3:1 (pulmonary overcirculation)
Target SaO2 75-85% in HLHS to balance Qp:Qs near 1:1
2. Phases of Shock
2.1 Compensated Phase
Neuroendocrine mechanisms maintain vital organ perfusion at expense of nonvital organs
BP normal, ↑HR; cold extremities, ↓urine output, prolonged CRT → nonvital organ signs
2.2 Uncompensated Phase
Compensatory mechanisms exhausted → hypotension → ↓CO → lactic acidosis
All organs including vital organs compromised
2.3 Irreversible Phase
Multiorgan failure; interventions ineffective
3. Pathogenesis of Neonatal Shock
3.1 Hypovolemia
Absolute: hemorrhage (fetoplacental, fetomaternal, cord avulsion), capillary leak
Relative: vasodilation (sepsis, NEC, asphyxia, lusitropes like milrinone)
Delayed cord clamping → ↑blood volume → ↓vasopressor use (ACOG 2020, NRP 2020)
Cord milking → ↑IVH risk in <32 wk (Katheria JAMA 2019) → NOT recommended
3.2 Myocardial Dysfunction
Systolic: postasphyxial, viral myocarditis, septic shock late phase
Diastolic: IDM with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, pericardial tamponade
Structural CHD: HLHS, critical coarctation, critical AS → "sepsis-like" ductus closing
3.3 Abnormal Vasoregulation
Vasodilatory shock: sepsis, NEC, severe asphyxia, ECMO, RDS of prematurity
Chorioamnionitis/funisitis → hypotension + hyperdynamic cardiovascular compromise at birth
Vasopressor-resistant hypotension (≤27 wk): excess NO, KATP channel activation, ↓adrenergic receptors → relative adrenal insufficiency
4. Developmental Regulation
4.1 Blood Pressure Norms
Nuntnarumit Clin Perinatol 1999: nomogram 103 neonates 23-43 wk; each line = 10th percentile mean BP for GA group
GA-specific (first 72 hr): 23-26 wk ~20-25 mmHg; 27-32 wk ~25-35 mmHg; 33-36 wk ~30-40 mmHg; 37-43 wk ~35-45 mmHg
Clinical rule: mean BP (mmHg) < GA (weeks) = hypotension threshold
3 definitions: (1) epidemiologic (<10th percentile); (2) autoregulatory threshold; (3) ischemic threshold
4.2 CBF Autoregulation
Even very preterm neonates have CBF autoregulation but narrow range
CO2-CBF reactivity robust: 1 mmHg ↑PaCO2 → ~4% ↑CBF
Forebrain (cortex, basal ganglia) behaves like nonvital organ in very preterm → cerebral ischemia despite "normal" BP
Ischemia-reperfusion → P/IVH: Noori J Pediatr 2014 — low SVC flow → reperfusion → P/IVH
4.3 SVC Flow
Normal range: 40-120 mL/kg/min (median 70 rising to 90 mL/kg/min by 48 hr)
SVC flow used when PDA+PFO shunting makes LVO/RVO unreliable for systemic perfusion
LVO = systemic + ductal flow; RVO = systemic + atrial shunt → neither = true systemic flow when shunts present
5. Diagnosis of Circulatory Compromise
5.1 Clinical Signs
CRT: ≤3 sec = normal (but CRT 4.23 ± 1.47 sec in healthy newborns first 72 hr!)
CRT >5 sec + lactate >4 mmol/L → specificity 97% for low SVC flow (Miletin 2009)
Core-peripheral temperature difference: poor correlation with SVC flow in <30 wk
Urine output: low in first 24 hr (normal physiology) → not useful then; after day 2-3 more useful
Serum lactate: represents PAST events; rising lactate more predictive than single value
pH/base excess: little correlation with SVC flow in immediate postnatal period
5.2 NIRS
rSO2 (regional O2 saturation): brain, kidney, intestine, muscle
Renal rSO2 declines before hypotension or acidosis → earlier intervention opportunity
Cerebral rSO2: preserved initially (compensated); eventually falls in decompensated
Used in: cardiac surgery, PDA management, asphyxia, ECMO
5.3 Functional Echocardiography
LVO normal: 150-300 mL/kg/min; RVO normal: 150-300 mL/kg/min
RVO better systemic measure in first 1-2 days (before significant PFO shunt)
Intraobserver variability ~10%, interobserver ~20% (mostly from vessel diameter measurement)
TAPSE, RVGLPS: predictors of death/ECMO in PPHN (Malowitz 2015)
6. Treatment
6.1 Volume Administration
Indication: absolute/relative hypovolemia, sepsis, post-op
First choice: isotonic saline 10-20 mL/kg IV
Albumin 5%: ≥effective as saline in acute setting (Lynch 2008 RCT); but ↑mortality concern, ↑cost
Balanced crystalloids (LR, Plasma-Lyte A): lower Cl → less hyperchloremic acidosis; emerging evidence
Caution: aggressive fluid in VLBW → ↑BPD, NEC, IVH, death (Van Marter 1990)
6.2 Vasopressor-Inotropes

rows: ↑TSH inhibition; check thyroid screen after use
Best: myocardial dysfunction + ↑SVR; Cochrane: dopamine > dobutamine for BP
↑Lactate (glycogenolysis) — not reliable lactate monitoring during use
Drug of choice adult septic shock; limited neonatal data

blocks.text: 6.3 Hydrocortisone — Vasopressor-Resistant Hypotension
Rationale: (1) normalize BP quickly; (2) relative adrenal insufficiency in VLBW; (3) glucocorticoids → restore adrenergic receptor expression
Non-genomic effect: ↑BP within 2 hr; Genomic: ↓vasopressor need 8-12 hr (Seri Pediatrics 2001)
Dose: 1 mg/kg IV q8-12h (physiologic replacement, not pharmacologic)
CRITICAL: indomethacin + hydrocortisone concurrent → ↑spontaneous intestinal perforation (avoid week 1)
Long-term: 2 placebo-controlled trials showed NO ↑cerebral palsy with low-dose early HC (Baud JAMA 2017)
Strength of evidence: would need 74 studies showing no effect to overturn benefit (Higgins 2010 meta-analysis)
6.4 ECMO
VV-ECMO: oxygenation support only (PPHN, ARDS)
VA-ECMO: cardiac support (refractory septic shock, myocarditis, post-cardiac surgery, severe IDM HCM)
Overall neonatal survival ~50% for cardiac indications; ~73% all indications (ELSO 2021)
GBS sepsis ECMO survival: 71% (Schlapbach 2020)
Surviving Sepsis Campaign (Weiss ICM 2020): VV-ECMO for refractory PARDS; VA-ECMO for refractory septic shock
7. NICU Practical — Shock Protocol at NICU
ทารกหัวใจเต้นเร็ว + BP ต่ำ + CRT >3 sec → define type of shock: volume? myocardial? vasodilatory?
Step 1: Functional echo — LVO/RVO, ventricular function, LA:Ao, ductal shunt direction, PDA size
Step 2: ถ้า hypovolemia → NS 10 mL/kg IV bolus ×1-2; ประเมิน response; ถ้าไม่ตอบสนองใน 2 bolus → vasopressor
Step 3: dopamine เริ่ม 5 mcg/kg/min titrate; ถ้า SVR สูง (cold shock + poor CO) → เพิ่ม dobutamine / milrinone
Step 4: ถ้า vasopressor dose >15-20 mcg/kg/min ไม่ตอบสนอง → consider hydrocortisone 1 mg/kg IV (ตรวจ basal cortisol ก่อนถ้าทำได้)
Step 5: ตรวจ NIRS cerebral + renal rSO2; ถ้า renal rSO2 ลด → ↑CO priority; ถ้า cerebral rSO2 ลด → ↑perfusion pressure
Step 6: ประเมิน ALCAPA, HLHS, critical CoA ถ้า not improving → PGE1
Warning: dopamine → ↑TSH suppression → เลื่อน thyroid screen; epinephrine → ↑lactate (artifact, not tissue hypoperfusion)
8. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — BP < GA in weeks ≠ Always Treat, But Always Assess
mean BP (mmHg) < GA (weeks) = epidemiologic hypotension threshold; แต่ compensated shock อาจมี BP ปกติ; ดู clinical context: urine output, lactate, NIRS, echo ก่อนตัดสินใจ รักษา. ไม่ใช่ทุก BP ต่ำที่ต้องให้ยา — ดู perfusion markers

PEARL 2 — LVO ≠ Systemic Blood Flow ในช่วง Transitional Period
ในช่วง PDA open: LVO = systemic + ductal flow → overestimation; ใช้ RVO หรือ SVC flow แทน SVC flow ปกติ 40-120 mL/kg/min; Kluckow & Evans ADC 2000

PEARL 3 — Dopamine ↑ Risk ต่อ TSH — อย่าลืม Thyroid Screen
dopamine ↓ TSH + T4 70-80% และ 50% ตามลำดับ (Filippi ADC 2007); thyroid screen ต้องทำ หลัง dopamine หยุดแล้ว ≥24 hr; ถ้าทำระหว่างให้ dopamine → false low TSH → delayed hypothyroidism diagnosis

PEARL 4 — Hydrocortisone + Indomethacin = Contraindicated Week 1
concurrent use → ↑spontaneous intestinal perforation (mostly ileal); avoid unless basal cortisol ต่ำมาก; ถ้าจำเป็น use แยก timing อย่างน้อย 2 wk; เป็น absolute contraindication ในสัปดาห์แรกของชีวิต

PEARL 5 — Epinephrine → Lactate ↑ ไม่ใช่เพราะ Tissue Hypoperfusion
epinephrine stimulates glycogenolysis → ↑serum lactate independently of cardiovascular status; อย่าใช้ lactate เป็น sole marker of response ขณะให้ epinephrine → ใช้ NIRS, urine output, echo แทน
9. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Durrmeyer X et al. Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed. 2017 (EPIPAGE 2): N=119 treated vs 119 untreated isolated hypotension in extremely preterm. Treated group: ↑survival without severe morbidity, ↓IVH. Dose-effect: defining hypotension as mean BP 5 mmHg below GA → even stronger benefit.
Kluckow M, Evans N. Arch Dis Child 2000: SVC flow normal range 40-120 mL/kg/min; low SVC flow → IVH; SVC flow novel marker systemic perfusion in transitional period with open fetal channels.
Noori S, McCoy M et al. J Pediatr. 2014;164:264: Changes in cardiac function and CBF in relation to P/IVH in extremely preterm infants. Ischemia → reperfusion pattern before P/IVH detected.
Pellicer A et al. Pediatrics. 2005;115:1501: Dopamine vs epinephrine RCT in VLBW, both ↑BP + CBF; neurodevelopmental follow-up at 2-3 yr: no difference in morbidity.

Seri I, Tan R, Evans J. Pediatrics. 2001;107:1070: Hydrocortisone cardiovascular effects in preterm with vasopressor-resistant shock. BP ↑ within 2 hr (non-genomic); dopamine ↓ within 8-12 hr (genomic).

Noori S, Friedlich P et al. Pediatrics. 2006;118:1456: Low-dose HC in vasopressor-treated neonates. Improvement in all hemodynamic parameters: BP, SVR, SV, LVO, HR — confirms hemodynamic benefit not just BP.

Subhedar NV, Shaw NJ. Cochrane 2000 (CD001242): Dopamine vs dobutamine for hypotensive preterm. Dopamine more successful raising BP; fewer treatment failures. No difference neurologic short-term outcomes.
Paradisis M et al. J Pediatr. 2009;154:189: Milrinone vs placebo for low systemic blood flow in VLBW — no improvement in SVC flow. Suggests low SVC flow not solely due to ↑SVR.

Katheria A et al. JAMA. 2019;322:1877 (cord milking RCT): N=474, <32 wk. Cord milking vs delayed cord clamping. Cord milking → ↑severe IVH/death. Cord milking NOT recommended despite hemodynamic benefits.
Rabe H et al. Cochrane 2019 (CD003248): Delayed cord clamping vs immediate. Delayed: ↑blood volume, ↓transfusions, ↓IVH, ↑stability. ACOG 2020 + NRP 2020 support delayed cord clamping.

Higgins S, Friedlich P, Seri I. J Perinatol. 2010;30:373: Meta-analysis hydrocortisone for hypotension. 74 and 188 future negative studies needed to negate benefit on BP and vasopressor reduction, respectively.
Baud O et al. JAMA. 2017;317:1329: Early low-dose hydrocortisone in extremely preterm: ↑survival without neurodevelopmental impairment at 2 yr compared to placebo. No ↑cerebral palsy.

Weiss SL et al. Intensive Care Med. 2020;46(Suppl 1):10 (Surviving Sepsis Campaign children): Early fluid resuscitation in neonatal/pediatric sepsis ↓mortality. VV-ECMO for PARDS; VA-ECMO rescue for refractory septic shock.
Miletin J, Pichova K, Dempsey EM. Eur J Pediatr. 2009: Bedside detection low systemic flow in VLBW day 1. CRT >4 sec + lactate >4 mmol/L → specificity 97% for low SVC flow.

Nuntnarumit P, Yang W, Bada-Ellzey HS. Clin Perinatol. 1999;26:981: Blood pressure measurements in newborn. Nomogram 103 neonates 23-43 wk, first 3 postnatal days. Defines 10th percentile mean BP for each GA group — still standard reference.
10. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: ทารก GA 26 wk, 18 hr of life, mean BP 20 mmHg, CRT 4 sec, lactate 3.5, urine output 0.5 mL/kg/hr — ควรทำอะไร?

ตอบ: Mean BP 20 < GA 26 = hypotension; CRT 4 sec + lactate 3.5 (ใกล้ threshold 4 mmol/L) → ประเมิน functional echo ก่อน; ถ้า low SVC flow หรือ poor LV function → เริ่ม dopamine 5 mcg/kg/min; ให้ NS 10 mL/kg ถ้า echo แสดง hypovolemia; NIRS monitoring
Q: อธิบาย 3 phases of shock และ clinical signs แต่ละ phase

ตอบ: (1) Compensated: BP normal, ↑HR, cold extremities, ↓UO, ↑CRT — compensatory mechanisms intact, nonvital organ hypoperfusion; (2) Uncompensated: BP ↓, ↓CO all organs, lactic acidosis — mechanisms exhausted; (3) Irreversible: MOF, interventions ineffective
Q: ทำไม LVO ไม่ใช่ gold standard ของ systemic blood flow ใน VLBW ในช่วงแรก?

ตอบ: ใน transitional period ที่ PDA และ PFO เปิดอยู่: LVO = systemic flow + L→R ductal shunt → overestimate; RVO = systemic + atrial shunt → ก็ไม่ accurate ถ้า PFO significant; SVC flow ดีที่สุดในช่วงนี้ เพราะไม่ได้รับผลจาก ductal shunting; normal 40-120 mL/kg/min
Q: Dopamine ออกฤทธิ์อย่างไรตาม dose?

ตอบ: Low dose (2-5 mcg/kg/min): DA1 receptors → renal/mesenteric vasodilation; Medium (5-10): β1 → ↑contractility, ↑HR; High (10-20+): α1 → ↑SVR, ↑BP; ใน critically ill neonates, receptor downregulation may require higher doses; dopamine ↑TSH suppression — delay thyroid screening
Q: ข้อบ่งชี้ของ hydrocortisone ใน cardiovascular compromise?

ตอบ: Vasopressor-resistant hypotension = dopamine >15-20 mcg/kg/min without adequate response; relative adrenal insufficiency suspected; mechanism: glucocorticoids → ↑adrenergic receptor expression, ↑vascular smooth muscle contractility (mineralocorticoid), ↑capillary integrity; dose: 1 mg/kg IV q8-12h; AVOID concurrent with indomethacin (week 1 → ↑intestinal perforation)
Q: Cord milking vs delayed cord clamping — ทำอย่างไรถูกต้องในปัจจุบัน?

ตอบ: Delayed cord clamping ≥30-60 sec (ACOG 2020, NRP 2020) = RECOMMENDED สำหรับ preterm ทุกราย; ↑blood volume, ↓IVH, ↓transfusions; Cord milking: Katheria JAMA 2019 RCT — ↑severe IVH/death ใน <32 wk → NOT recommended; mechanism unclear, possibly rapid ↑CBF in vulnerable forebrain
Q: ทารก GA 28 wk มี BP ปกติ แต่ renal rSO2 ลด, cerebral rSO2 ยังปกติ — interpret อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Compensated shock: nonvital organs (kidney) showing early hypoperfusion; vital organ (brain) protected; renal rSO2 ลดก่อนอาการทางคลินิก → early sign; ควรประเมิน echo, lactate, urine output; ถ้า renal rSO2 ยังต่ำ → intervene ก่อนที่จะ decompensate; cerebral rSO2 may fall later as compensatory mechanisms exhaust
Q: ECMO indication ใน neonatal cardiovascular compromise?

ตอบ: VA-ECMO: refractory septic shock (myocardial dysfunction), acute myocarditis, post-cardiac surgery low CO, severe IDM HCM; VV-ECMO: PPHN with respiratory failure; overall indication: OI >40 (respiratory), refractory hemodynamic instability unresponsive to maximal medical therapy; overall neonatal cardiac ECMO survival ~50% (lower than respiratory ~73%); GBS sepsis ECMO survival 71%
Q: อธิบาย ischemia-reperfusion mechanism ในการเกิด P/IVH ใน preterm

ตอบ: (1) Initial ↓CO/↓SVC flow → cerebral ischemia (low rSO2, high CFOE — Noori J Pediatr 2014); (2) Forebrain vessels behave like nonvital organ (vasoconstrict) in very preterm; (3) Recovery of CO → reperfusion → pressure-passive CBF (impaired autoregulation) → vascular rupture → P/IVH; factors that exaggerate reperfusion: permissive hypercapnia >50-55 mmHg, rapid vasopressor response, cord milking
Q: Milrinone — role ใน neonatal shock คืออะไร?

ตอบ: Milrinone = PDE3 inhibitor → ↑cAMP → inodilator (↑contractility + ↓SVR); best indication: LV dysfunction + ↑SVR (e.g., post-cardiac surgery, IDM HCM); RCT (Paradisis J Pediatr 2009): milrinone did NOT prevent/improve low SVC flow in VLBW in transitional period; adverse effects: hypotension (worst in HIE + hypothermia + iNO), thrombocytopenia (42% infants); use with caution in preterm (↑risk IVH from hypotension)
Day 47 Preview

พรุ่งนี้: Persistent Pulmonary Hypertension of the Newborn (PPHN)
ต่อเนื่องจากวันนี้ที่เรียนเรื่อง cardiovascular physiology; เราจะมาดู failure of pulmonary vascular transition specifically. เนื้อหาหลัก: NO-cGMP pathway, PGI2-cAMP pathway, iNO (FDA-approved, 20 ppm), OI calculation + ECMO threshold (OI >40), CDH complexity, BPD-PH, และ adjunct therapies (sildenafil, milrinone, bosentan, treprostinil). เชื่อมกับวันนี้: management of right ventricular dysfunction + systemic hemodynamics ใน PPHN context.
